title: Intracranial EEG During Near-Threshold Detection: All-or-None Ignition Dynamics
description: Intracranial EEG (iEEG) recordings in epilepsy patients performing a near-threshold visual detection task test whether conscious access produces all-or-none (bistable) firing rate distributions in the frontoparietal network, as predicted by APGI's ignition criterion Bₜ. Four sub-predictions are tested: that ignition produces bimodal (not graded) firing rate distributions in frontoparietal iEEG at detection threshold (P6a), that bimodality is specific to frontoparietal electrodes while occipital units show graded responses (P6b), that autocorrelation coefficient AC1 in pre-ignition high-gamma power increases prior to ignition events (P6c, the bifurcation falsification criterion distinguishing APGI from standard GWT), and that near-threshold stimuli can produce stable intermediate firing rate states lasting > 100 ms (P6d). SUBMISSION-BLOCKING: APGI_LNN_Bifurcation_Analysis.py required for full iEEG pipeline and is not yet built. P6c is the critical distinguishing APGI prediction over standard GWT and must appear verbatim in Appendix C.3 of Paper 2.

sub_predictions.claim: Ignition produces bimodal (not graded) firing rate distributions in frontoparietal iEEG at detection threshold
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: Bimodal histogram of single-trial high-gamma power (70–150 Hz) in frontoparietal electrodes at near-threshold contrast; Hartigan's dip test p < 0.05 for bimodality
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: Unimodal continuous distribution of firing rates across all contrast levels; no evidence of bistability

sub_predictions.claim: Bimodality is specific to frontoparietal ignition network; occipital units show graded responses
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: Hartigan's dip test significant for frontoparietal electrodes (p < 0.05) but not occipital/early visual electrodes (p > 0.2); significant region × distribution-shape interaction
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: Uniform bimodality across all recorded regions, or uniform graded responses regardless of region

sub_predictions.claim: Critical slowing precedes ignition events: AC1 of pre-ignition high-gamma power increases
sub_predictions.critical_note: THIS IS THE BIFURCATION FALSIFICATION CRITERION distinguishing APGI from standard Global Workspace Theory. Must appear verbatim in Appendix C.3 of Paper 2. GWT predicts threshold-crossing without critical slowing; APGI predicts AC1 increase reflecting approach to bifurcation point.
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: Autocorrelation coefficient AC1 of high-gamma envelope (70–150 Hz) increases monotonically in the 500 ms window preceding ignition events (Kendall τ > 0.3, p < 0.05); effect absent in non-detected trials
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: No AC1 trend in pre-ignition window; AC1 flat or decreasing before detected stimuli

sub_predictions.claim: Near-threshold stimuli produce stable intermediate states lasting > 100 ms before resolving to detected or non-detected outcome
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: Significant proportion of near-threshold trials show sustained intermediate firing rate (25–75% of bimodal peak separation) for > 100 ms before committing to high or low state; prevalence > 15% of near-threshold trials
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: Rapid commitment to high or low firing state within 50 ms; no sustained intermediate states exceeding 100 ms duration

validation_notes: Empirical pipeline for clinical iEEG not yet standardized. APGI_LNN_Bifurcation_Analysis.py required for automated iEEG state detection, bimodality testing, and AC1 computation pipeline — NOT YET BUILT. This is submission-blocking for Paper 1 and Paper 2.

inclusion_criteria: Adult epilepsy patients (age 18–65) with clinically indicated intracranial electrode implantation for seizure localisation
Electrode coverage of frontoparietal cortex (required: at least 4 electrodes in prefrontal and parietal cortex bilaterally)
Cognitive capacity to perform 2AFC near-threshold detection task (verified by neuropsychologist)
Seizure-free period of ≥ 48 hours at time of testing
exclusion_criteria: Active seizure within 48 hours of testing session
Significant cognitive impairment precluding reliable task performance (MMSE < 24)
Electrode placement limited to mesial temporal structures without frontoparietal coverage
Visual field defect covering stimulus region
Sedating medication (benzodiazepines, phenobarbital) within 24 hours of session

apparatus.iEEG_system: Natus Quantum or Blackrock Microsystems NeuroPort; sampling rate ≥ 2000 Hz; 0.3–600 Hz hardware bandpass
electrode_types: Depth electrodes (SEEG): Ad-Tech or PMT Corporation, 8–16 contacts, 3.5 mm spacing
Grid/strip electrodes: Ad-Tech, platinum-iridium, 4 mm contact diameter, 10 mm spacing
target_regions_required: Prefrontal cortex (dlPFC, vlPFC): bilateral coverage preferred
Occipital/early visual cortex (V1/V2 for regional comparison, P6b)
apparatus.display: Laptop screen at 60 cm viewing distance, 120 Hz refresh rate, gamma-corrected
apparatus.stimulus_delivery: Psychtoolbox-3 with photodiode verification; TTL trigger to iEEG system
apparatus.eye_tracking: Eyelink 1000 Plus (monocular, 500 Hz) for fixation verification

target.type: Gabor patch, vertical orientation, foveally presented
target.near_threshold_contrast: Individual threshold determined by adaptive QUEST procedure in 50-trial pre-session

trial_structure.response: 2AFC (seen / not seen), response window 1500 ms, button press with non-dominant hand

preprocessing: Notch filter: 60 Hz and harmonics (US) or 50 Hz and harmonics (EU)
Common average re-referencing per electrode array
Manual rejection of ictal and interictal discharge epochs
ieeg_analysis_pipeline.high_gamma_extraction: Bandpass filter + Hilbert analytic signal; amplitude envelope at 10 ms resolution
ieeg_analysis_pipeline.bimodality_test: Hartigan's dip test on single-trial high-gamma amplitude distribution at near-threshold contrast; significance threshold p < 0.05

ac1_analysis.description: Autocorrelation coefficient (lag-1) of high-gamma envelope in 500 ms pre-ignition window
ac1_analysis.ignition_event_definition: Single trial classified as detected (seen); near-threshold contrast only
ac1_analysis.trend_test: Kendall τ across consecutive 50 ms windows in pre-ignition epoch

intermediate_state_detection.definition: Single trial high-gamma amplitude between 25% and 75% of bimodal peak separation for ≥ 100 ms

ieeg_analysis_pipeline.regional_comparison: Mixed-effects model: bimodality index (dip statistic) ~ region (frontoparietal vs. occipital) + contrast + (region | participant)

dependent_variables: Hartigan's dip statistic: frontoparietal high-gamma distribution per contrast level
Hartigan's dip statistic: occipital high-gamma distribution (regional comparison, P6b)
AC1 time series: high-gamma envelope in 500 ms pre-ignition window (P6c — bifurcation criterion)
Kendall τ for AC1 trend: detected vs. non-detected trials
Proportion of near-threshold trials showing stable intermediate state > 100 ms (P6d)
Hit rate and d-prime per contrast level (behavioural)
High-gamma onset latency: detected vs. non-detected trials per region
primary_hypothesis: High-gamma power in frontoparietal iEEG shows a bimodal (not graded) distribution at near-threshold contrast, and autocorrelation coefficient AC1 in the pre-stimulus high-gamma band increases systematically in the 500 ms preceding detected stimuli (ignition events), consistent with critical slowing near a bifurcation point as predicted by APGI and not predicted by standard Global Workspace Theory.
primary_analysis: Hartigan's dip test on single-trial high-gamma amplitude distributions (one per electrode cluster per participant). AC1 trend: Kendall τ across 50 ms windows; comparison between detected and non-detected trials (permutation test, 10,000 permutations). Regional specificity (P6b): linear mixed-effects with region × bimodality-index interaction.

power_analysis.software: Custom bootstrap (rare iEEG populations preclude standard power calculators)
power_analysis.based_on: Schurger et al. (2012) bistability in iEEG; King et al. (2013) all-or-none frontoparietal dynamics

apgi_parameters.bistability_criterion: Hartigan dip p < 0.05 in frontoparietal electrodes

paper_2_appendix_requirement.verbatim_text: Prediction P6c (AC1 increase in pre-ignition high-gamma) constitutes the bifurcation falsification criterion distinguishing APGI from standard Global Workspace Theory. Standard GWT predicts threshold-crossing as a deterministic event without precursor dynamics. APGI additionally predicts that approach to the ignition bifurcation produces measurable critical slowing, indexed by increased autocorrelation (AC1) in the pre-stimulus high-gamma band. Failure of P6c would not falsify APGI's core threshold-crossing account but would eliminate the bifurcation interpretation of ignition dynamics.
paper_2_appendix_requirement.status: Must be inserted verbatim before Paper 2 submission

ethics.data_sharing: De-identified iEEG and behavioural data to be deposited on OpenNeuro after publication

submission_blocking_issues: APGI_LNN_Bifurcation_Analysis.py: Automated iEEG state detection, Hartigan's dip test pipeline, and AC1 computation script is NOT YET BUILT. This is submission-blocking for Paper 1 and Paper 2.
iEEG analysis pipeline has not been validated on pilot data. At minimum one pilot participant's data should be processed through the full pipeline before finalising preprocessing parameters.
Appendix C.3 of Paper 2 (verbatim bifurcation falsification criterion for P6c) has not yet been written. Must be completed before Paper 2 submission.
caveats: iEEG patient populations have limited sample sizes; statistical power is constrained. Single-participant case series may supplement group analysis.
Anti-epileptic drugs (AEDs) may suppress high-gamma activity; AED type and dose should be reported as covariates.
Electrode placement is determined by clinical need, not experimental design; frontoparietal coverage cannot be guaranteed for all participants.
Stable intermediate states (P6d) require precise timing; photodiode-verified stimulus onset TTL accuracy < 5 ms is mandatory.
